skipped.reason: paramiko 3.5.1 already includes the Terrapin attack fix (strict KEX extension support added in paramiko 3.4.0). The pyproject.toml constraint >=3.4.0 enforces this minimum. Verified strict KEX code (kex-strict-*-v00@openssh.com) is present in paramiko 3.5.1 transport source at lines 2453, 2543, 2546. The scanner's version-to-CVE mapping is incorrect for this package version.

structured_output.reasoning: CVE-2023-48795 (Terrapin SSH prefix-truncation attack) was fixed in paramiko 3.4.0 with strict key-exchange extension support. The repo installs paramiko 3.5.1 (pinned in requirements/base.txt:279 and requirements/development.txt:650), which is above the fix version. The pyproject.toml (line 91) enforces paramiko>=3.4.0,<4.0, guaranteeing the fix floor. I verified the strict KEX negotiation code is present in the installed paramiko 3.5.1 source (paramiko/transport.py lines 2453, 2543, 2546). paramiko is used in superset/extensions/ssh.py for SSH tunnel connections via Transport.start_client() — exactly the code path where the Terrapin mitigation applies. This is a false positive: the scanner flagged the package without recognizing that version 3.5.1 already contains the fix. Posted verification findings as a comment on issue #6 (https://github.com/michaelszhu/superset/issues/6#issuecomment-4783453126). Note: the source_issue_url (#101) does not exist; #6 is the actual tracking issue.
